guarantees.requirement: Participant count is between 2 and 19 when tournament is active or finished
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 1 implies 2 <= |m.participants| <= 19 whenever phase != Setup. Guarantee_ParticipantCount extracts this from Inv.

guarantees.requirement: Total rounds is at least 1 when tournament is active or finished
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 1 implies totalRounds >= 1 whenever phase != Setup. Guarantee_TotalRoundsMin extracts this from Inv.

guarantees.requirement: Current round count never exceeds total rounds
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 5 directly states CurrentRound(m) <= m.totalRounds. Guarantee_CurrentRoundBound extracts this.

guarantees.requirement: Participants have non-negative scores at all times

coveredBy.expression: forall i: int {:trigger m.participants[i]} :: 0 <= i < |m.participants| ==> m.participants[i].score2x >= 0

guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 0 states all participant scores are >= 0. Guarantee_ScoresNonNegative extracts this from Inv.

guarantees.requirement: Opponent tracking is symmetric across all participants (FIDE Rule 2 foundation)
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 6 includes OpponentsSymmetric, which means if i has j as opponent then j has i as opponent. Guarantee_OpponentsSymmetric extracts this.

guarantees.requirement: Accepted round pairings contain no rematches between participants (FIDE Rule 2)
guarantees.reasoning: NoRematchesWhenValid requires ValidPairingsForModel and ensures NoRematchPairings, directly proving that valid pairings contain no rematches.

guarantees.requirement: Each participant appears at most once in accepted round pairings (FIDE Rule 3)

coveredBy.expression: forall i, j :: 0 <= i < j < |pairings| ==> pairings[i].whiteIdx != pairings[j].whiteIdx && pairings[i].whiteIdx != pairings[j].blackIdx && pairings[i].blackIdx != pairings[j].whiteIdx && pairings[i].blackIdx != pairings[j].blackIdx

guarantees.reasoning: Guarantee_ParticipantAppearsOnce makes participant-level uniqueness explicit: no participant index can appear in more than one pairing slot across the entire round.

guarantees.requirement: A bye is present when and only when the participant count is odd (FIDE Rule 3)
guarantees.reasoning: ByeIffOddWhenValid requires ValidPairingsForModel and ensures the biconditional: bye exists iff participant count is odd.

guarantees.requirement: Accepted bye recipient has not previously received a pairing-allocated bye (FIDE Rule 4)
guarantees.reasoning: ByeRecipientEligibleWhenValid requires ValidPairingsForModel and bye.Some?, then ensures the bye recipient has not previously received a bye.

guarantees.requirement: Active round always has structurally valid pairings and valid participant indices
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 9 includes ActiveRoundValid(m), ensuring the active round has valid pairings, distinct indices, and valid bye. Guarantee_ActiveRoundValid extracts this.

guarantees.requirement: Color history length equals total games played as white plus black for every participant
guarantees.reasoning: Inv conjunct 8 includes ColorHistoryConsistency, which expands to |colorHistory| == whiteCount + blackCount for every participant. Guarantee_ColorHistoryConsistency extracts this.

guarantees.requirement: Color difference for every participant stays within minus 2 to 2 (FIDE Rule 6)

coveredBy.expression: -2 <= ColorDiff(p1) + (if c1 == White then 1 else -1) <= 2 && -2 <= ColorDiff(p2) + (if c2 == White then 1 else -1) <= 2

guarantees.reasoning: ChooseColorsPreservesColorDiff proves that after color assignment, both participants' color differences remain in [-2, 2], provided no conflicting hard constraints exist. Scope: per-operation postcondition for each pairing's color choice.

guarantees.requirement: No participant receives the same color three times consecutively (FIDE Rule 7)
guarantees.reasoning: ColorAlternationPreservesNoThree proves that if history has no three consecutive same colors and the new color does not form a third consecutive, then the extended history still satisfies NoThreeConsecutive. Scope: per-operation postcondition for each color assignment.

guarantees.requirement: All accepted pairings cover every participant exactly once (bye or paired)

coveredBy.expression: 2 * |pairings| + (if bye.Some? then 1 else 0) == |m.participants| && (forall i, j :: 0 <= i < j < |pairings| ==> pairings[i].whiteIdx != pairings[j].whiteIdx && ...) && (bye.Some? ==> bye.value < |m.participants| && (forall p :: p in pairings ==> p.whiteIdx != bye.value && p.blackIdx != bye.value))

guarantees.reasoning: Guarantee_FullCoverage combines count equation, participant-level distinctness across all pairings, and bye exclusion — together proving every participant is covered exactly once.
